scope_note: GPT-2 family (3 models) only. Encoder-only models (BERT) skip in decode mode; T5 enc-dec decode is TODO. Single-token forward with KV cache from prompt prefill.

data.config_grid: 8 batches × 6 seqs × 3 models × 5 GPUs (seq=1024 skipped on decode mode for headroom)

comparison_to_prefill.note: Decode mode is *easier* to predict for graph-aware models because decode latency is dominated by weight-bytes-read (memory-bound, very repetitive across configs) while prefill latency mixes compute and memory regimes.

cross_gpu_observations: gpt2-small/medium/large per-token decode latencies scale ~3× from small to large (vs ~6.2× param ratio) — memory-bandwidth-limited rather than compute-limited
B200 vs H100: ~2× faster on decode, tracking the ~2.4× HBM bandwidth ratio (8 TB/s vs 3.35 TB/s)

A100-SXM4-40GB anomalously *slower* than A10 on decode despite 2.6× more bandwidth — suggests eager-mode attention dispatch quirk on Ampere; consistent with the existing prefill A100 numbers being similarly slow for small batches
open_questions: Why is A100-SXM4-40GB slower than A10 on decode? Investigate whether torch.eager attention takes a slow path on Ampere SM_80
T5 (enc-dec) decode plumbing: needs decoder_input_ids + cached encoder cross-attention — TODO
T4 and H200 Modal jobs still queueing as of 2026-04-29 17:38 EDT; data not yet collected on those 2 GPUs
Mixed-split (per-sample routing) experiment: needs ≥4 models for meaningful held-out + held-config test; can't run yet with only 3 GPT-2 models
